title: Venmo's Biggest Makeover In Years Comes At A Very Interesting Time
text: Venmo is launching its most significant overhaul since 2021, with changes rolling out this week and continuing through fall 2026. The redesigned app introduces a visually rich feed featuring larger images, expanded interaction options like reactions and quick-action buttons, and personalized cashback offers from brands. The update includes three additional tabs for improved navigation: Send Tab displaying frequent contacts and simplifying bill-splitting with groups up to 30 people; Money Tab centralizing expense management, Teen Accounts, and cryptocurrency features; and Rewards Tab consolidating limited-time offers and Stash cash-back rewards. Users can now endorse local businesses through a Give a Shoutout button, addressing demand from younger audiences wanting to support merchants. The timing carries significance beyond routine improvements. PayPal is restructuring Venmo as a standalone unit, with observers interpreting this as preparation for divestiture. Reports indicate Stripe has expressed acquisition interest in PayPal itself. Venmo's product lead noted that research revealed how many features customers are unaware exist. The redesign reflects broader shifts toward younger users expecting payment platforms to function as social experiences comparable to Revolut and Daylight.
